items.why: SCOPED 2026-07-10 — NOT a clean bug, a DESIGN QUESTION (do not autonomously build). items.title: Bethpage 7/11 guide-validation conflict: DIAGNOSED — carry-tolerance vs bunker footprint (decision needed)
items.status: diagnosed — decision needed (do NOT inline-fix: validator is the anti-injection/grounding control)
items.why: INVESTIGATION 2026-07-09 (cycle 39, OFFLINE from committed fixture tests/fixtures/bethpage_overpass.json — same data prod ingested; no prod SSM needed). Holes 7 & 11 were persistently honest-empty (research ~4x each fails validate_guide). ROOT CAUSE = (c) validator over-strict, NOT (a) geometry or (b) OSM. EVIDENCE: (a) ruled out — extract_hole_hazards gives correct sides/carries matching reality: hole 7 par-5 dogleg-right (polyline 346->36->55deg) bunkers R 170/430/520 + L 355/525; hole 11 par-4 bunkers both sides L 245/415 R 270/325/420. All normal/correct non-numbered guide phrasings PASS the validator. (b) ruled out on-hole — 7/11 have ONLY bunkers, nearest water 395-431y OFF the played line (not in play); a water/OB mention is therefore a CORRECT rejection (OSM has no 'ob' polygon type so any OB phrase always rejects — expected). (c) CONFIRMED + reproduced: _side_and_carry_supported() checks a claimed carry vs each bunker's CENTROID +-25y, but Bethpage bunkers are large — hole 7's right cross-bunker centroid=170y actually SPANS 103-280y (178y wide). A web-researched guide naturally citing any carry in the real footprint (220/240/250/260) but >25y from centroid -> WHOLE guide REJECTED (demonstrated: all four reject). That is why grounded, reasonable numbered advice keeps failing = the persistence pattern. DECISION NEEDED (genuine product/risk call, not an obvious fix): (c1) widen the carry check to the bunker's surveyed near->far carry SPAN (recovers guides on big-bunker holes) — but this loosens the grounding/anti-laundering control that had a bypass closed just days ago (5e4b861 carry-tie-break laundering), so it REQUIRES the full adversarial treatment: Fable plan + eval-harness teeth (each new accept-case proven RED pre-fix) + adversarial review proving no false number laundering re-opens; vs (c2) accept status quo — honest-empty is SAFE and correct (caddie falls back to the grounded generic hazard line); large-bunker holes simply get no cached prose guide. Recommend (c1) as a properly-reviewed future build item IF cached guides on such holes are wanted; otherwise close as working-as-designed. Optional confirmation: an on-box research pass capturing the exact rejected text would pin which number/phrase fired in prod, but the local reproduction already demonstrates the mechanism — not worth an unattended prod SSM action. FILES: backend/app/caddie/guide_writer.py (_side_and_carry_supported, _has_side_flip, _CARRY_TOLERANCE_YARDS=25); backend/app/caddie/hazards.py (extract_hole_hazards, centroid-based carry). FORBIDDEN to inline-fix per tasks/lessons.md (geometry/validation change without full adversarial treatment).

OWNER 2026-07-09 thesis, researched (specs/glasses-shot-tracking-research.md): continuous-vision tracking INFEASIBLE (60-90min battery, 1-min clip caps, thermal shutoffs) but TRIGGERED-CLIP design is plausible — IMU/impact-audio trigger -> 5-10s 720p clip via Meta Wearables DAT (dev preview; 12MP cam + 5-mic + speakers accessible to OUR phone app) -> on-phone swing/ball classification, fused with GPS displacement. ~5-7 min total camera/round = inside battery. Replaces vocal confirmation with VISUAL — the owner's objection to phone-only sensing dissolves. Putts = first sensor with a real chance, but trigger reliability unproven. SHIPPING gated on Meta opening publishing (their 2026 date); PROTOTYPING possible now. STEP 0 ($0, today): owner pairs Ray-Ban Metas as BT audio with the live caddie — phone-free conversation works without any code. Spike = diagnostic-only, measure trigger precision/recall + battery on owner rounds before any UX.

items.title: Per-user rate limiting + spend cap on paid LLM endpoints
items.why: AUDIT 2026-07-08 (caddie-excellence-audit P1.2; area E = grade F). ZERO per-user rate/token/spend limits on the paid LLM endpoints — only guards are auth + a 4000-char input cap; an authed client can loop /voice/stream, /session/voice*, /realtime/session mint, /course-intel with no cost ceiling (the single biggest cost/scale risk, and it can drain the metered-overflow $50 cap). Add per-user RPM + TPM limits (enforce BOTH — request count alone misses one expensive prompt) and a hard monthly token/$ budget with 80/90% alerts; token-aware abuse flag (high-input/short-output = injection probing). Start single-box (in-process/Postgres counter); note Redis shared store is needed once we run >=2 workers (see caddie-multiworker, P2). Silent backend/infra. UNBLOCKED for single-box.

items.title: Timeouts + bounded retries on the Claude caddie calls
items.why: AUDIT 2026-07-08 (caddie-excellence-audit P1.3; area F). The Anthropic caddie calls pass no timeout/max_retries (routes/caddie.py:796,863,1359; memory.py:129) so they inherit the SDK ~10-min default — with --workers 1 a single hung call ties up the ONLY worker for minutes on-course (throughput cliff). Add explicit timeout (~20-30s) + bounded retries; degrade to calm in-character honest copy on exhaustion (matches the humanizeVoiceError / fail-to-honest-state rule). Contrast the already-bounded httpx timeouts (realtime mint 15s, TTS 30s). Cheap, high-value resilience. Silent. UNBLOCKED.

items.title: Caddie advice-quality eval harness (golden set + LLM-judge, CI gate)
items.status: done-on-bundle (PR #117, silent; f491b71 on integration/next; reviewer SOUND — harness honest/falsifiable, 3 non-blocking notes folded in; Tier-1 47 passed offline + full suite green + ruff clean). Two-tier caddie advice-quality eval: Tier-1 deterministic prompt-assembly+honesty asserts (always in CI, no key/DB) over a 25-scenario golden JSONL seeded from the real hole-4 incidents (no-left-bunker hallucination, observed-reality gaslight, Bethpage-4 side-flip) with a closed check-name registry + teeth tests proving every check goes RED on the pre-fix world; Tier-2 LLM-judge (judge!=candidate, injection-safe, budget-capped, projection-abort) is on-demand/nightly only, triple-gated off CI. Follow-ups: grow golden set 25->30-50 (incident-driven growth rule in README); optional nightly Tier-2 schedule workflow; optional extract _build_session_voice_prompt pure body so the text mouth is Tier-2-drivable offline.
items.why: AUDIT 2026-07-08 (caddie-excellence-audit P1.5; area G = grade D). No advice-QUALITY harness exists — caddie tests cover geometry/plumbing only, runtime LLM output has no automated quality gate, so 'good enough to replace a caddie' is unfalsifiable and every other caddie improvement is unmeasurable. Build a git-diffable golden JSONL (30-50 scenarios: hole geometry + player + question -> expected advice properties, incl. edge cases: dogleg, hazard carry, downhill lie, low-confidence parse). Two tiers per SOTA: deterministic assertions first (names a real club? sane yardage bounds? no hallucinated hazard? valid shape) + an LLM-JUDGE rubric ('grounded in the actual hole? accounts for stated lie/wind? appropriately concise?'); regression-gate in CI; de-bias the judge (judge with a DIFFERENT model than the one under test, randomize order, control for verbosity). Extends the existing offline voice-tests gate pattern. Silent. UNBLOCKED.

items.title: Clerk session persistence in iOS webview (standardBrowser:false)
items.rationale: Sign-in completed but session did not persist (isSignedIn stayed false -> bounced to signed-out) — iOS WKWebView blocks Clerk third-party session cookie. Awaiting owner: sign in -> stays -> force-quit -> still signed in.
items.note: ROOT CAUSE (matches evidence exactly): WebView CORS blocks reading the Authorization response header cross-origin (capacitor://localhost -> clerk.looperapp.org), so the session JWT is never captured (tok never flips). FIX: CapacitorHttp:{enabled:true} routes fetch through native NSURLSession which bypasses browser CORS + reads all headers. + richer copyable diagnostic (auth-hdr field = key signal). Building. WATCH: CapacitorHttp patches global fetch — verify the audio-blob transcribe upload + other API calls still work on-device. | Shipped v1.0.361 (build 202606280650). Awaiting owner: sign in -> auth-hdr/signed true? Copyable diagnostic added. | v1.0.361 diagnostic: native-sent:FALSE (window hooks never fire with CDN clerk-js) — CONFIRMED. Fix: bundle @clerk/clerk-js locally + register FAPI callbacks on the INSTANCE + pass Clerk={instance} to ClerkProvider (the @clerk/expo pattern). Building. | Shipped v1.0.365 (build 202606280936): local @clerk/clerk-js instance + instance callbacks. Awaiting owner: native-sent should now be true. | CRASH ROOT CAUSE: bundled Clerk instance had no UI components -> <SignIn> mountSignIn assertComponentsReady throws -> white-screen. FIXED (d78de56): standard CDN ClerkProvider + guarded window hooks; app loads (sim-verified). NATIVE-AUTH ROOT CAUSE: clerk-react@5/clerk-js@5 dont honor the token hooks -> native-sent never fires. REAL FIX: upgrade clerk-react->v6 (clerk-js@6 honors hooks + ships UI). Building v6 upgrade. Autonomous testing now exists: frontend/ios/SIMTEST.md + simtest-headless.mjs (CI-able crash check). | VERIFIED 2026-06-28 via REAL credentialed sign-in in iPhone-17 sim (WebKit remote inspector + ios-webkit-debug-proxy): @clerk/react 6.11.1 / clerk-js 6.22.0. ALL native-auth signals GREEN: native-sent=true on EVERY FAPI request incl. sign_ins POST/attempt_first_factor (v6 honors window hooks); auth-hdr=true + tok=true (CapacitorHttp made authorization response header readable, JWT captured + persisted to Preferences); napi=true. Password (first factor) ACCEPTED. signed=false reached ONLY because Clerk gated the new device behind an emailed second-factor OTP ('signing in from a new device') which the agent can't read (no inbox) — Clerk product security, orthogonal to native-auth plumbing. CONCLUSION: native-auth fix CONFIRMED working; remaining step is the human entering the email OTP on first sign-in. Shipped verified build v1.0.369 (build 202606281037).

items.rationale: Security review of v1.0.343 (auth wiring otherwise SOUND). Three items, bundle as one pass before production: (1) store __clerk_client_jwt in a Keychain-backed plugin (@aparajita/capacitor-secure-storage or capacitor-secure-storage-plugin) with kSecAttrAccessibleWhenUnlockedThisDeviceOnly so it is excluded from device/iCloud backups (currently @capacitor/preferences = UserDefaults plaintext, backup-exposed — Medium); (2) the AuthProvider comments at :50 and :117-118 + the commit msg FALSELY claim Keychain — correct them; (3) on Clerk sign-out, Preferences.remove/secureStorage.remove the JWT (hooks currently never clear it -> stale credential persists after sign-out). items.title: Swap native JWT storage backend from UserDefaults to Keychain plugin
items.rationale: Final part of clerk-jwt-keychain. Replace @capacitor/preferences (UserDefaults, plaintext, backup-exposed) with @aparajita/capacitor-secure-storage using kSecAttrAccessibleWhenUnlockedThisDeviceOnly so the Clerk JWT is encrypted at rest + excluded from device/iCloud backups. Thanks to native-token-store.ts this is a single-file backend swap (+ add the plugin + cap sync). NOTE: adds a native dependency (SPM/pod) and the actual Keychain persistence can only be fully verified on a real device/sim sign-in -> bundle with other device-verifiable changes or verify via a credentialed sim run before shipping. REQUIRED before App Store / wider release; NOT beta-blocking (owner-only TestFlight).
review_followups: LOW (TOCTOU, reviewer 2026-06-28): an in-flight FAPI response can re-persist the JWT just after clearNativeToken() on sign-out. Security-nil (Clerk destroys the session server-side; re-persisted token is revoked). Fix when doing the Keychain swap: gate setNativeToken on a module-level 'signed out' flag that clearNativeToken sets and a sign-in transition (false->true) resets — taking care NOT to suppress the re-sign-in token write (it happens during the sign_in FAPI exchange, before isSignedIn flips).

LOW (cold-start stale token, reviewer 2026-06-28): if sign-out happens while the app is killed, the stale (already server-invalid) JWT isn't cleared until it's injected and rejected. Fix: in nativeOnAfterResponse, clearNativeToken() on a FAPI authentication-failure error code — but first confirm the EXACT Clerk FAPI error code via a credentialed run so a valid token is never wrongly wiped.

items.rationale: The preload-removal hotfix (cd2e516) stopped background hallucination, but the root noise/silence-hallucination risk remains because the mint uses whisper-1, no noise reduction, and raw server_vad. VERIFY exact field names against the OpenAI Realtime API reference before shipping (an unsupported transcription model breaks the mint). Owner-testable on device. If wind robustness still inadequate after this, evaluate a Krisp-equipped Deepgram pipeline (LiveKit/Pipecat) — see notes.

items.title: Fix voice-setup chat ordering — user message shows AFTER the caddie's reply
items.rationale: OWNER HIGH PRIORITY (2026-06-28): owner says 'hello', caddie replies, but the caddie's reply renders ABOVE the 'hello'. Cause: the user transcript event (conversation.item.input_audio_transcription.completed) arrives AFTER the assistant's streamed response deltas, and messages render in arrival/insertion order (VoiceRoundSetupRealtime upsert appends new ids in arrival order). Fix: order messages by conversation-item sequence, not arrival — the user's input item is created BEFORE the response item. In realtime.ts handleEvent, capture an ordering key (item creation order / a monotonic seq, or use the item_id ordering the API provides) and sort the rendered list by it; keep streamed assistant deltas coalescing. Add a unit test for the ordering helper.
items.progress: 2026-06-28: built 179d03c on integration/next. New pure MessageOrderTracker + sortByOrder (lib/voice/realtime-ordering.ts): a stable monotonic order key assigned when each conversation ITEM begins (user slot reserved at input_audio_buffer.speech_started, identity-matched by item_id to the transcript; assistant slot at response.created/first delta), NOT when transcript text arrives. realtime.ts: RealtimeMessage gains required `order`, handleEvent threads item_id, sendText emits the typed line centrally + renders even if DC closed. Both renderers sort by order (VoiceRoundSetupRealtime + useRealtimeCaddie). Reviewer adversarial pass: found + fixed a real desync (FIFO user-slot matching corrupted ordering for the rest of the session on phantom/empty/VAD-bounced speech_started -> re-keyed by item_id; added the regression test). 9 unit tests. Gates: lint 0/0 / tsc 0 / voice-tests 265/265 / vitest 374/374 / build clean. DEVICE-ONLY verify: WebRTC live voice ordering must be confirmed on the next TestFlight build. NOTICEABLE -> rides the next approval bundle.

items.title: Fix sheet grabber-handle drag — swiping it scrolls the background page instead of dismissing
items.rationale: OWNER (2026-06-28): swiping DOWN on a sheet's grab handle moves the background page instead of dismissing the sheet. The drag isn't being captured at the handle, so the gesture falls through to page scroll. Audit the drag-to-dismiss sheets (CaddieSheet + the new VoiceRoundSetupRealtime grab handle): ensure the handle's onPointerDown calls dragControls.start(e), the draggable motion.div has the dragControls + dragListener=false wired, and the background/body can't scroll under the sheet (lock body scroll / overscroll-behavior: contain / touch-action on the backdrop) while a sheet is open. Verify on device.
items.progress: 2026-06-28: built 52a1d9a on integration/next. Audit found BOTH sheets already had the handle wired correctly (onPointerDown -> dragControls.start, touch-action:none, dragListener=false, drag y + dragControls on the motion.div). Root cause of the fall-through was the MISSING piece: nothing locked the page behind an open sheet, so any gesture framer-motion didn't claim scrolled the background (esp. iOS WKWebView document rubber-band, which touch-action alone doesn't suppress). Fix: new src/lib/sheet.ts with useBodyScrollLock(locked) (pins <body> position:fixed + overflow hidden + overscroll-behavior none, restores scroll on close) and the pure shouldDismissSheetDrag(offsetY, velocityY) threshold (>120px OR >600px/s) extracted from both sheets. Wired into CaddieSheet (locked on `open`) and VoiceRoundSetupRealtime (locked for mounted lifetime); added touch-action:none + overscroll-behavior:contain to both backdrops. Inner conversation list stays scrollable. 8 unit tests (src/lib/sheet.test.ts: threshold edges + lock apply/restore via jsdom). Gates: lint 0 / tsc 0 / voice-tests 265/265 / vitest 382/382 / build clean. DEVICE-ONLY verify: the drag gesture is pointer/touch-only — confirm on next TestFlight that the handle dismisses and the page no longer scrolls behind an open sheet. NOTICEABLE -> rides the next approval bundle.

items.progress: 2026-06-28: built 727c7df on integration/next. ROOT CAUSE (hypothesis 1, evidence-backed): the Realtime remote audio sink in realtime.ts was document.createElement('audio')+autoplay but NEVER appended to the DOM and had no inline-playback attr. Authoritative iOS guidance (webrtcHacks Safari guide, Capacitor #8176): iOS WKWebView renders remote WebRTC audio through a SINGLE attached element and 'doesn't support simultaneous playing of multiple elements'; a detached autoplay element can leave the track to ALSO render via the audio session -> two slightly-offset copies = 'two overlapping voices'. FIX: make the element the SOLE controlled sink -> append to document.body (hidden), setAttribute('playsinline',''), autoplay, idempotent srcObject assign (only on a different stream), and audioEl.remove() in cleanup() so reconnects/warm-preloads never stack a second sink. start() is guarded (if(this.pc)return) and its error path calls cleanup() -> at most one element per client (verified: hook + VoiceRoundSetupRealtime each hold one clientRef, recreate only after stop()). DEFENSIVE rule-out (hypothesis 2): NO double-response path -> response.create fires only on typed input (sendText) and after a tool result (runTool); minted session uses server_vad with no create_response:false, so voice turns auto-respond exactly once. Mint config untouched (owner: deployed+working). Reviewer (fresh opus context): SOUND/ship, no blocking issues; confirmed no double-sink, SSR-safe, defensive claim holds. Non-blocking nit (pre-existing, NOT the cause, left out of scope): onconnectionstatechange doesn't call cleanup() on a silent network drop. Gates: lint 0 / tsc 0 / voice-tests 265/265 / vitest 399/399 / build clean. DEVICE-ONLY verifiable (audio) -> MUST confirm on next TestFlight build the caddie no longer plays two overlapping voices. NOTICEABLE -> rides the next approval bundle (PR #66).
items.title: Caddie voice plays TWICE (two overlapping voices) on every response
items.rationale: OWNER 2026-06-28 (voice is otherwise much better now): when the caddie responds, two overlapping voices play. Setup uses ONE RealtimeCaddieClient, so NOT two sessions. Top suspects, in order: (1) the WebRTC remote audio element in realtime.ts is created with document.createElement("audio")+autoplay but NEVER appended to the DOM and has no playsInline — on iOS WKWebView the remote track can auto-play via the audio session AND the element → two offset copies. FIX candidate: append the audioEl to document.body (display:none), set playsInline + autoplay, and ensure it is the SOLE sink; remove on cleanup. (2) double response per turn — verify only ONE response.created fires per user turn (no stray response.create; confirm server_vad create_response not combined with an explicit create; confirm interrupt_response cancels an in-progress response so a new turn does not overlap an old one). (3) stale session/audioEl not torn down on reconnect. Device-verify with the owner. HIGH priority.

items.title: Voice telemetry events dropped on iOS (flush on suspend)
items.status: done-shipped-main (rider, shipped via early bundle to main)
items.why: Observed 2026-07-08: voicetel volume is near-blind (~1 event in 4h of a live session). lib/voice/telemetry.ts flushes on an 8s timer / 12-event batch / visibilitychange->hidden with keepalive fetch. On iOS WKWebView (Capacitor), pagehide is more reliable than visibilitychange, and the CapacitorHttp-patched fetch may not honor keepalive when the webview suspends -> queued events die on background/kill before the timer fires. Add a pagehide/freeze listener + verify keepalive actually ships (or use sendBeacon / a smaller batch / immediate flush for rare high-signal events). Don't fix while we're mid-TTS fix; targeted follow-up.
items.resolution: Cycle 19 (1c65b49, rides PR #109). Option A — smallest secure design, no new/unauth surface: added a `flush?:boolean` flag to voiceEvent() that immediately drains the WHOLE queue, wired it on all failure events (mic_error/live_start_failed/live_unsupported/resolved_fallback/speak_failed/prime_failed) so they ship while foregrounded; added a window pagehide listener alongside visibilitychange. Authenticated fetch(keepalive) + Clerk Bearer preserved; sendBeacon/token-in-body rejected (attack surface). Reviewer CLEAN + security-reviewed (auth preserved, no PII, flag never in payload); 10 deterministic jsdom tests. Unblocks device-side latency verification.
